rationale.root_cause: They identified the unsafe copy at line 13 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained that argv input can exceed the 256-byte destination, causing a stack buffer overflow. They explicitly called it a stack-buffer-overflow and tied it to argv[1].
rationale.local_fix: They replaced strcpy with strncpy/snprintf and ensured null-termination but did not propose rejecting overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejecting oversized input in addition to bounded copy and null-termination.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing raw, unbounded string handling by using dynamically sized buffers and bounded string APIs (e.g., std::string/strncpy_s), which addresses the root cause. This aligns with carrying capacities/using bounded APIs across the data path.
